args.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS patients_with_encounters, SUM(CASE WHEN provider_count = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS patients_with_one_provider, SUM(CASE WHEN provider_count > 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS patients_with_multiple_providers FROM (SELECT patient_id, COUNT(DISTINCT provider_id) AS provider_count FROM encounters GROUP BY patient_id) x;

SELECT COUNT(*) AS patients_with_encounters, SUM(CASE WHEN provider_count = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS patients_with_one_provider, SUM(CASE WHEN provider_count > 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS patients_with_multiple_providers FROM (SELECT patient_id, COUNT(DISTINCT provider_id) AS provider_count FROM encounters GROUP BY patient_id) x;

SELECT COUNT(*) AS patients_with_encounters, SUM(CASE WHEN provider_count = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS patients_with_one_provider, SUM(CASE WHEN provider_count > 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS patients_with_multiple_providers FROM (SELECT patient_id, COUNT(DISTINCT provider_id) AS provider_count FROM encounters GROUP BY patient_id) x;

SELECT pr.provider_id, pr.name AS provider_name, pr.specialty, COUNT(DISTINCT fu.patient_id) AS overdue_patient_count
FROM follow_ups fu
JOIN encounters e ON e.patient_id = fu.patient_id
JOIN providers pr ON pr.provider_id = e.provider_id
WHERE fu.completed_at IS NULL
  AND fu.due_date < CURRENT_DATE
GROUP BY pr.provider_id, pr.name, pr.specialty
ORDER BY overdue_patient_count DESC, pr.name
LIMIT 10;

SELECT pr.provider_id, pr.name AS provider_name, pr.specialty, COUNT(DISTINCT fu.patient_id) AS overdue_patient_count
FROM follow_ups fu
JOIN encounters e ON e.patient_id = fu.patient_id
JOIN providers pr ON pr.provider_id = e.provider_id
WHERE fu.completed_at IS NULL
  AND fu.due_date < CURRENT_DATE
GROUP BY pr.provider_id, pr.name, pr.specialty
ORDER BY overdue_patient_count DESC, pr.name
LIMIT 10;

SELECT pr.provider_id, pr.name AS provider_name, pr.specialty, COUNT(DISTINCT fu.patient_id) AS overdue_patient_count
FROM follow_ups fu
JOIN encounters e ON e.patient_id = fu.patient_id
JOIN providers pr ON pr.provider_id = e.provider_id
WHERE fu.completed_at IS NULL
  AND fu.due_date < CURRENT_DATE
GROUP BY pr.provider_id, pr.name, pr.specialty
ORDER BY overdue_patient_count DESC, pr.name
LIMIT 10;

message.prose:
I treated “overdue for follow-up” as `follow_ups.completed_at IS NULL` and `follow_ups.due_date < CURRENT_DATE`, and attributed each patient to their provider via `encounters.provider_id`.

### Providers with the most overdue follow-up patients

| Rank | Provider | Specialty | Overdue patients |
|---:|---|---|---:|
| 1 | Allison Hill | Cardiology | 60 |
| 2 | Abigail Shaffer | Orthopedics | 5 |
| 2 | Angelica Tucker | Dermatology | 5 |
| 2 | Angie Henderson | Pediatrics | 5 |
| 2 | Anthony Humphrey | Oncology | 5 |
| 2 | Anthony Rodriguez | General Practice | 5 |
| 2 | Brian Ramirez | Neurology | 5 |
| 2 | Brittany Farmer | General Practice | 5 |
| 2 | Carla Gray | General Practice | 5 |
| 2 | Cassandra Gaines | Psychiatry | 5 |

Allison Hill has the most overdue follow-up patients by a wide margin, with 60.

Source query returned 10 rows:

```sql
SELECT pr.provider_id, pr.name AS provider_name, pr.specialty,
       COUNT(DISTINCT fu.patient_id) AS overdue_patient_count
FROM follow_ups AS fu
JOIN encounters AS e ON e.patient_id = fu.patient_id
JOIN providers AS pr ON pr.provider_id = e.provider_id
WHERE fu.completed_at IS NULL
  AND fu.due_date < CURRENT_DATE
GROUP BY pr.provider_id, pr.name, pr.specialty
ORDER BY overdue_patient_count DESC, pr.name
LIMIT 10
```

I also checked provider attribution consistency: all 500 patients with encounters had exactly one distinct provider in the `encounters` table. That validation query returned 1 row.

citation.sql: SELECT pr.provider_id, pr.name AS provider_name, pr.specialty, COUNT(DISTINCT fu.patient_id) AS overdue_patient_count FROM follow_ups AS fu JOIN encounters AS e ON e.patient_id = fu.patient_id JOIN providers AS pr ON pr.provider_id = e.provider_id WHERE fu.completed_at IS NULL AND fu.due_date < CURRENT_DATE GROUP BY pr.provider_id, pr.name, pr.specialty ORDER BY overdue_patient_count DESC, pr.name LIMIT 10
